title: Warning Dots in DataTable · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2334 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Feature Description Warning dots that appear in a row when a threshold is exceeded or criteria is met. Nice to have would be on hover, showing the reason the specific item exceeded the threshold. Challenging part of this will be how to structure the criteria. Even using a simple threshold is not straightforward because we would need to know if above or below triggers the warning. I think the simplest way to implement this would be: Require user to include a boolean column in their query which evaluates the rule For cell-level: Column accepts a warning prop, which is a reference to a column containing the boolean info When true, a circle is added next to the data within the column For row-level: DataTable also accepts a warning prop When true, a circle is added on the far left or right of the row If this logic works for warning dots, it can also be applied to other forms of conditional formatting - e.g., highlight a full row. Cell-Level Warning Dot Row-Level Warning Dot

Makes it much easier for custom formatting too || This is great for SLA reports. title: Metrics and/or Data Documentation Visible to Developers and End Users · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2656 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Description From this slack thread The TL;DR is that developers and end users can see how data and SQL powering the Evidence app is defined. Like Looker's ? info icon that showed SQL and user defined descriptions on hover in the interface. What problem would this solve? It would save a lot of time, effort, and accuracy if developers and end users could see how data is defined from the interface instead of either not knowing or having to ask around. For example, if in the data warehouse sql some label bucketing column values is defined, or the SQL powering an Evidence chart has a where clause, the end user is not going to know what goes into each bucket or chart filter unless the developer explains so in the Evidence markdown (or the end user is savvy enough to view all the sql queries which may not be realistic). However, any hard coding of column explanations would mean the Evidence app is effectively outdated as soon as it is deployed, so ideally there is something more 'programmatic' in this sense. How should it work? It would be great to be able to hover over a column in Evidence and see some kind of lineage or data explanation be presented. For example if a column is defined with a case statement and the sql powering it is something like when column_a in ('x', 'y', 'z') then 'label_i' else 'label_j' end then the end user should be able to view that quickly without having to go digging through the view SQL queries themselves. I find myself wanting something like dare I say a LookML-like semantic layer...or at the very least the ability to write a piece of logic and reuse it like a dbt macro i.e. define a 'data_labelled' constant in a way that can write the sql and also the markdown using the same piece of reusable code like a variable. Then the sql and the markdown will always be linked.

title: Waterfall Chart · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2372 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: A waterfall chart is great for showing decomposition of changes. I've had a start at creating one (which I will open in a draft PR for those who are interested in taking a look). Considerations for implementation Axis labels are important on these charts and will often be long strings - the chart needs to be able to handle multi-line labels without cutting off characters or breaking words apart I think it probably needs "breaking" behaviour by word, followed by either truncation ( ... ) or expansion of the chart size This will also need to work well responsively and on mobile We don't currently have multi-line axis labels in our other charts - this will need some thinking to make sure the chart area is the right size in all cases. Currently, adding multi-line axis labels shrinks the main chart area The initial attempt I've made requires a specific data format: category - category to use on x-axis value - change to show on chart total - boolean - should this be treated as a total/subtotal on the chart The SQL required to get data into this format can require a lot of work. We might want to offer other alternatives for the structure of the data getting passed in I don't think this would change the implementation within echarts - we could just add a layer of logic above this to get it into the format echarts requires Data labels should be included - positioning these may be difficult I haven't thought about tooltips yet. I'm thinking they will be straightforward though - probably a config override sent from Waterfall to Chart Subtotals - the logic for generating the chart series will need to be updated to accomodate subtotals - currently, having a total in the middle of the data throws off the cumulative sum We may want to offer sorting - e.g, sort by magnitude of change. This shouldn't be hard to do with this data format since we can easily identify the totals and exclude them from sorting In a lot of cases, people need to start the y-axis at a higher number than 0 to make the changes visible enough on a waterfall chart We could offer a yMin prop to allow someone to set it manually I could also imagine some dynamic logic to check the change range (max of cumulative sum - min of cumulative sum) as a % of axis range and adjust the yMin so that the change range takes up something like 50% of the vertical chart area
author: hughess
comment_excerpt: The SQL required to get data into this format can require a lot of work. We might want to offer other alternatives for the structure of the data getting passed in I think including a sample SQL query in the documentation would go a long way. It is indeed fairly complex but you can simplify it massively with clever windowing (LAG / LEAD or cumulative sums). I think having a clean example of the data model on this one is key because it always takes me like an hour in power BI to get these right. || @matsonj Does the below interpret what you are saying correctly? There are a few common uses for waterfalls, for example Revenue bridge (by category, geography etc) Volume value bridge (revenue start, impact of price, impact of volume, impact of mix, residual, revenue end) We should write an example of how to wrangle a standard table format in order to do a waterfall of the above types eg Assuming your columns are this: date , revenue , category Then use this example query to get into waterfall format Assuming your columns are this date , item , price , quantity , [total revenue] Then use this other query And as a follow up question: what are your most typical waterfall use cases (assuming not covered by the above two)? || @archiewood yeah, I think those are the core cases. I think the core questions I usually run into that usually are answered best with waterfalls are the following: Why did revenue change this month vs last in percentage and absolute $? (i.e. change in revenue by type - recurring, churn, growth, etc) Why did profitability change this month vs last month in percentage and absolute $? (i.e. contribution margin by product line) Could go down the line on any GL line item too, but those are the two that seem the most frequent in my exp. || Is Waterfall still on the Backlog, or is it closed for now? 😊 || Still on the backlog, would definitely welcome contributions! || Awesome! Unfortunately, I don't think I have the abilities to contribute... 😅

title: Custom Aggregate Functions in Data Table Component · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2491 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Description I think the Data Table component needs the option to specify custom aggregation functions or formulas to display accurate total and subtotal rows. What problem would this solve? Without the ability to specify a custom aggregation function, some aggregations cannot be calculated accurately. For example, take percent change: ( new_value - old_value ) / old_value I have a data table with four columns: product_name sales sales_year_ago sales_yoy_percent_chg (derived from sales and sales_year_ago using the percent change calculation in sql) The aggregate in the total row correctly sums the sales and sales_year_ago columns, but none of the existing aggregation functions correctly display sales_yoy_percent_chg. The sales and sales_year_ago columns need to be aggregated with sum first, and then the calculation above applied. Applying a mean or weighted mean aggregation to the product percent change values does not yield the correct result. EDIT: I forgot to add that custom aggregation values is a solution for tables with a single total row, but does not work for the unique aggregations necessary in a groupBy subtotal. How should it work? No response
author: nickfanion
comment_excerpt: On Slack, Sean pointed out to me that setting weightCol to old_value instead of new_value will aggregate percent change values correctly with totalAgg set to weightedMean . Custom aggregation functions might still be a useful feature for other use cases, but I haven't run into others that can't be solved by the built-in aggregation functions. || Thanks @nickfanion ! We've got an issue open to improve the documentation for this. Thanks for flagging it

title: Localized number formats (separators) · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2519 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Description Would be nice to see a way to implement localization for number formats with regards to separators. What problem would this solve? Currently it is possible to use fmt=eur2 on a number 1234.56 to see it formatted as €1,234.56. But in Germany it should actually read 1.234,56 € . So the thousands separator is a dot and the separator for decimals is a comma. Also not that the currency symbol is at the end. In Switzerland, this number should read 1'234.56 € with an apostrophe as thousands separator and a dot for the decimals separator. I am not sure if this can be done with the current formatting options. At least, I did not succeed using the prop fmt='#.##0,00" €"' on a DataTable column. It is possible to format the number through SQL DuckDB 1.1.0: D select format ('{:t.} €', 1234.56); and get the string 1.234,56 € . However, all number related props and charts will no longer work on this column. How should it work? Ideally, keep the format and specify locale.

author: jaroet

title: On demand refresh for near-realtime use cases · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2380 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Feature Description A "refresh" button in the Web UI, which would queue up an on-demand refresh. Goal of Feature This could help with use cases that require near-realtime data, by allowing Evidence to get the latest data without having a window into the backend via SSH, and without acruing query costs of refreshing a full set of data every n minutes. This might be used in combination with a daily refresh or n-times daily. Variations / Implementation options Where the button might be: This could be put on a special "Admin view" page. This could be at the top of each rendered page, hidden under an exapandable "dataset on this page" widget. This could be part of the expandable data section per each chart. What the button might do: Could refresh one selected dataset and only the active page. Could refresh one selected dataset and all impacted pages of the site. Could refresh all the datasets on the active page. Could refresh all the datasets on the site. Atomic or global: This on-demand refresh could leave other pages/visuals alone, versus also auto-updating other pages or other visuals on the current page, versus refreshing the site anywhere that dataset is rendered. Bonus: For transparency reasons, it becomes even more helpful to have a place on the page that renders "last updated on..." per dataset, and/or for the page overall. (Harder to do if not all visuals on the page have the same refresh time.) Current Solution Today I think the best solution is to refresh every n minutes. Alternative Ideas Alternatives include: Refreshing every n minutes on a schedule. (Noted above.) Define certain queries "on-demand" whenever the page loads. Not mutually exclusive with this option, but the refresh-on-demand approach balances flexibility along with render performance; whereas an on-demand query would add significant lag. Automatically queuing data refreshes when a page reaches a certain staleness threshold, along with a notification to the user that the refresh has been queued. This saves the user from needing to push a button, and is cheaper than running every n minutes, but is more complex overall, and may be better implemented on top of on-demand refreshes, as a future iteration. Examples / Screenshots Not the same, because this is the Admin view, but Power BI has a feature like this in their web UI: Show/Hide Are you interested in contributing this feature? In theory 'yes', but I don't have capacity as of now, and I am not yet well-versed enough on the underlying codebase to do so efficiently. Related Is there any mechanism to control the update of the chart? #534
author: aaronsteers
comment_excerpt: @aaronsteers thanks for opening, always appreciate your input 🙏🏼 A couple of bits of context for anyone who is not intimately familiar with how Evidence works. How Evidence currently works Evidence has two "modes" Development (dev): the experience that a developer uses when building a project Build for production (prod): the experience that an end user of evidence sees online Unlike a lot of tools, these modes operate differently with respect to connecting to data Development There is a live connection to the data warehouse Queries only run for the page you are currently accessing in the browser Queries run live against the database Queries data is cached for x minutes (currently 15mins), unless the query is changed Production Evidence's build process generates a static site All queries in || Thanks, @archiewood ! Yeah, great context - thank you. 🙏 I especially like this part, and would like to keep it for production applications: All updates to the site are behind version control, and changes to the database will never break the live site Given that, I think any kind of on-demand refresh or near-realtime features should ideally fail back safely to the last-valid dataset. And very handy for transparency if something like a warning icon with tooltip message (or similar) could appear if any post-build refresh attempts have failed, such as an on-demand refresh request. || @aaronsteers something like this is becoming feasible as we make progress on #768 and #973 . It'll be down the road though. You can play with a prototype version here: https://github.com/evidence-dev/template/tree/next The above supports multiple simultaneous data sources, which can be updated outside of the process of building the front-end, which does open up a whole new set of possibilities, including near realtime, different schedules for different sources, incremental source refreshes, and snapshotting source data. When we bring this to main, the initial versions of the new build/ refresh data will just be for dev mode, but you can track progress on that here: #988 || @mcrascal Any updates for this scenario now that Universal SQL is launched? Can dynamically updating Parquet files (or directory) be reflected in charts without any manual steps to update?

title: Improve the queries directory to better fit w/ USQL · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2406 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Feature Description The details of the queries/ directory are a bit of a hold-over from pre USQL. The way they work now is kind of incongruent w/ the mental model of USQL, and takes you down a path of writing really non-performant pages. Queries in the queries directory help you accomplish two things: Cross source: Light data modelling across all your sources (e.g. joining the results of a postgres query to a snowflake query) Building light pipelines (e.g. a small dag of queries) Quick refactor target for queries you're repeating in pages. Copy/paste, keep the same syntax. Even if you would eventually want to full refactor the query into your source. Today, there are two issues "Importing" queries in front matter, and having them rendered on the page encourages users to put very large unaggregated queries on the page, which is a performance drag You need to have a query on the page in order to use another one which references it. Here's how I think they should work: Frontmatter references should not be required Queries in queries/ should only be able to reference sources and other queries in the directory. When we're compiling a chained query on a page, we should first check on the page for a query of that name, then check the queries directory, and swap in the compiled results from the queries directory.

author: hdulay
comment_excerpt: Hey @hdulay , Thanks for the suggestion! We're currently reworking our project to allow "pluggable" connectors , and I think pinot is a really good candidate for this. Context: currently, when we add connectors, we have to add them to the core library. This makes maintaining them a pain, as we (Evidence team) do not always have instances of these DBs to test. This new architecture will mean it's much easier for people to contribute and maintain these without being held back by the bandwidth of our team! If you're open to it, I can update here once the new architecture is live (hopefully pretty soon!) || Yes I look forward to it. Thanks || Hey @archiewood I'm also really interested in a Pinot connector! Were you able to make progress on the plugin architecture? || Hey sorry for the delay! We have totally re-architected Evidence since this issue was opened, and Evidence is indeed now "pluggable", meaning you can write your own connectors, publish them, and use them with Evidence. Here are some resources that may help: Docs on creating a plugin: https://docs.evidence.dev/plugins/creating-a-plugin/datasources Template for a connector plugin: https://github.com/evidence-dev/datasource-template An example that I spun up for pulling stats from the npm API: https://github.com/archiewood/npm-stats If you do write a Pinot plugin, the suggested package name is evidence-connector-[plugin] eg evidence-connector-pinot the docs are new, so let us know if there is extra information you need share it on the Evidence community , and we'll feature it! || Awesome thanks a lot @archiewood ! I'll have a look at this. || hey @cyrilou242 just heard from another user asking about pinot - did you ever look at this?

title: Progress logs for Templated Pages · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2429 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: For projects that lots of templated pages, there is no user feedback that they are loading. title: custom timeout for `mssql` connector · evidence-dev/evidence · Discussion #2414 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Feature Description Currently, it seems impossible to set a custom timeout when connecting through the mssql connector. When I have a query running more than 15000 ms, I get the following response when running npm run sources : The mssql connector uses npm package mssql under the hood . According to the npm docs , this package allows for setting custom requestTimeout and connectionTimeout , which both default to 15000 ms. Looking at the mssql connector source code, it seems there would be some opportunity for injecting custom requestTimeout and connectionTimeout somewhere here . I would suggest you just allow for a single timeout option, which steers both mssql timeouts under the hood, while still defaulting to 15000 ms. Goal of Feature Allow mssql users to run a query taking longer than 15s. Current Solution / Workarounds This is breaking my current process. Alternatives Not sure for now. I've already optimised the heck out of my queries. Other connectors offer larger timeouts already. Take a look at BigQuery 's 3,600,000 ms timeout .

author: archiewood
comment_excerpt: Should likely limit the content also or this is a potential to blow up huge tables, or cost a lot. Eg, imagine the costs of running this on a bigquery db with billions of rows. Could be quite expensive queries. If you use datagrip or some other editor they usually limit to some fixed size (500 rows) for you automatically behind the scenes when you do stuff. You could have a default limit, that is override-able tables: - orders - limit: 500 - reviews - limit: 0 #unlimited rows || Agree that this could be dangerous and we should think about how not to destroy people's DBs However also automatic (unspecified) limits can also be confusing and lead to errors in analysis (Easy to miss a dropped row if there were 505 rows in the table, and you excluded the last 5) An alternative would be to refuse to run tables above x rows (eg x=100,000) unless the user specifies limit=0 (or rows=all or similar), and throw a warning in this case? || Although now I think about it, that would require a count(*) operation on the table which is sometimes (depending on DBMS) an expensive operation

title: A New Chapter as a Community-Led Project · getredash/redash · Discussion #5962 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: The Redash project is now being rebooted as a community-led effort . The time has come for our community to actively participate in shaping Redash's development and direction. Although activity on our GitHub has been limited in the past year, Redash's adoption continues. To facilitate this transition, I reached out to individuals interested in maintaining the project, and the new team of maintainers currently has 7 volunteers. We encourage contributions from anyone, regardless of technical expertise. Whether you're a developer with coding skills or a user who can assist with testing, documentation, feedback, or other areas, your contribution is essential and valued. The future development of Redash will be entirely driven by the community. Our immediate focus is ensuring Redash's security and reliability by updating dependencies and improving Continuous Integration (CI) processes. Following that, the focus will be on adding features, fixing bugs, and continuing to develop Redash, but that will ultimately be up to the community. There is still much to accomplish beyond this announcement, such as determining the governance model. While I plan to remain as the BDFL for the time being, I envision day-to-day leadership emerging from the community. I am open to suggestions on this front. I acknowledge that this announcement may be lacking in specific details. Please feel free to ask questions, voice any confusion or concerns, and I will provide clarification in the comments.

title: Redash v26.3.0 released · getredash/redash · Discussion #7652 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: We are happy to announce the release of Redash v26.3.0 ! Release Notes Docker Tag: redash/redash:26.3.0 Upgrade from old versions: Apply migration using docker-compose run --rm server manage db upgrade
author: yoshiokatsuneo
comment_excerpt: I ran into this problem, noting here for others: If your docker compose file is more than five months old, it will try to upgrade to postgres 18 if you docker compose pull . You'll need to manually pin the version as in this PR: https://github.com/getredash/setup/pull/87/changes Other than that, smooth upgrade. || @robotmlg Thank you very much for your feedback report, and the information how to handle the case ! And yes, as Redash does not work well with PostgreSQL v18 or later for now, you need to pin the version to 17 (image: "pgautoupgrade/pgautoupgrade:17-alpine") . The recommended docker compose configuration is available below. https://github.com/getredash/setup/blob/master/data/compose.yaml || @yoshiokatsuneo what are the issues with newer pg versions? || @arikfr It looks, the PostgreSQL 18 changes the data directory from "/var/lib/postgresql/data" to "/var/lib/postgresql", causing the problem on volume mount as written in getredash/setup#86 and fixed in getredash/setup#87 .

comment_excerpt: As I see, there was a PR with this functionality earlier, but it was rejected #2011 || That would be helpful 👍 || If anyone on the Redash team is willing to work on this and get it done, I would be willing to discuss payment for the work - this feature is essential to my use case. It looks like it was rejected in #2011 , but that was years ago. Lets re-examine this please! || Agree... this would be really helpful, instead of giving access on underlying data sources. This would limit unnecessary access as well. || I think Dashboard-level access control may requires a new access control model that is bit different from current simple data source based model. Without good model, adding the feature may makes permission feature complex and can be the cause of miss configuration or bug and the security issue. So, to implement the feature, I think we need to well-designed access control model and UI that is easy to understand, and consistent through out the product. And, I think we are welcome contributors who lead the design and implementation. || For now, I think adding another data source limiting the access may work for some cases. How about ? || @abhijeetbold The only way right now is to give access to entire data source itself which is too wide. If the user needs to see data in the dashboard of a single table. How about just creating another "Redash data source" ? For example, you can create multiple Redash data sources for the same MySQL server. Each Redash datasource can have different MySQL users where one MySQL user have permission for full access and another MySQL user have permission to access only one table ? It might not be the best solution as we need to create different queries for different Data Sources. But it is somehow working in our organization , and I think it may work for some use cases. || @yoshiokatsuneo Your suggestion just pushes access control to somewhere it cannot be in my use case - I need to filter my queries based on a field within the table. I'm in a federated organization, so for example, I would want to display the contents of a table segmented by the result of the 'Member name' field inside the table. The member named John can only see John's data, and the member named Bob can only see Bob's data. I I can't do this using permissions in the database, because I need to give all users access to the field and table in order for them to be able to filter by its content, and in so doing I've already given them access to too much. What I need to do is create a Query that filters only John's data, and another query that filters only Bob's data, then give John access to  || @DregonX2 Thank you for your use case. If the data source is PostgreSQL, you may set low level security policy for each user and create Redash data source for each user. Or you may create views for each users... But yes, it depends on the data source, and may not be practical if there are many users... || So if I want to hire a developer to add more robust RBAC to Redash for a future release, do you have any advice on how I can help increase the chances that our code is accepted in to the main project? title: Zapier Integration broken — Redash app no longer appears in Zapier app search (self-hosted v25.8.0) · getredash/redash · Discussion #7667 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi everyone, I'm unable to connect Redash with Zapier for new users. The integration used to work (confirmed working ~2 years ago), but as of at least October 2025, new connections can no longer be made. Environment: Redash version: v25.8.0 (self-hosted) Zapier: standard plan Problem When a colleague tries to set up a new Zap and searches for "Redash" in the app selection screen (Trigger > Apps), Redash does not appear in the search results at all. Additionally, when following the official invitation flow described in the documentation: https://redash.io/help/user-guide/integrations-and-api/creating-a-zap/ The invitation link returns a 404 error with the message: "You seem to have floated away." This affects multiple colleagues. I (the original connector) can still use my existing Redash–Zapier connection without issues, but no new connections can be established. My hypothesis It seems like Redash may have been removed or deprecated from the Zapier app marketplace, which would explain both why it doesn't appear in search and why invitation links return 404. However, I haven't found any official announcement about this. Questions Has anyone else experienced this? Is Redash still officially listed on Zapier? Is there a known workaround for self-hosted Redash to integrate with Zapier (e.g., via Webhooks or the Redash API directly)? If the Zapier app has been deprecated, is there an official alternative or recommended migration path? Any help or insight would be greatly appreciated. Thanks!

title: Table Visualizations: Download as CSV includes hidden columns · getredash/redash · Discussion #6126 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: hi! I have a Query that pulls data for a data transfer, plus some internal fields that are used for other tasks. I have a Table Visualization set up that hides the internal fields, but the Download as CSV continues to download the full query results. To replicate: Create a new query with more fields than you want to download select a, b, c,d from t_table t limit 10 Save and Execute the Query. In the resulting Table Visualization, click the kebab menu and select [Download as CSV]. Your CSV contains the complete queryset with 4 columns. In the same table visualization, click [Edit Visualization], and uncheck the column you'd like to exclude. Save, of course. Kebab --> [Download as CSV] --> all fields are still included.

Deployment --- apiVersion : v1 kind : Service metadata : name : redash spec : clusterIP : None ports : - port : 5000 targetPort : 5000 selector : app : redash --- apiVersion : apps/v1 kind : Deployment metadata : name : redash labels : app : redash spec : replicas : 1 selector : matchLabels : app : redash template : metadata : labels : app : redash spec : containers : - name : redash-web image : redash/preview:latest ports : - name : redash-server containerPort : 5000 envFrom : - configMapRef : name : redash-env args : ["server"] - name : redash-worker image : redash/preview:latest envFrom : - configMapRef : name : redash-env env : - name : WORKERS_COUNT value : " 3 " args : ["worker"]  || See also a deployment posted by @emilburzo

title: I want to connect my mongodb hosted in Atlas using new feature called SQL interfce · getredash/redash · Discussion #7612 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: So far, we have been using the BI connector to Query in SQL, but it is being deprecated Link . Now that we have lots of queries in SQL, we want to use the SQL interface Link . I tried existing connectors, but they failed. So far, my understanding is that it would require a totally new connection driver. Any help or direction is appreciated.
author: MrSpark2591
comment_excerpt: Great question! MongoDB Atlas SQL Interface is indeed a powerful feature for running SQL queries against your document data. Here are some approaches that might help: Atlas SQL Interface Setup : Make sure you've enabled the Atlas SQL Interface in your cluster settings. You'll need to create a federated database instance that references your Atlas cluster. Connection String : The SQL interface uses a different connection string format than the standard MongoDB driver. You'll need the JDBC connection string from the Atlas SQL Interface settings, typically in format: mongodb:// : @ / ?authSource=admin Redash Integration : For Redash specifically, you might need to use the PostgreSQL or ODBC data source type since MongoDB Atlas SQL Interface speaks the PostgreSQL wire protocol. Query Migration || We tried ODBC and JDBC. Neither of them worked out. It seems it required a special driver provided by MongoDB.

Here are a few of the queries that were useful for monitoring if you need a sense of the internal schema: Usage: = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months') GROUP BY TO_CHAR(e.created_at, 'YYYY-MM') ORDER BY month DESC;"> SELECT TO_CHAR(e.created_at, 'YYYY-MM') AS month, COUNT(CASE WHEN e.object_type = 'dashboard' AND e.action = 'view' THEN 1 END) AS dashboard_views, COUNT(CASE WHEN e.object_type = 'query' AND e.action = 'execute' THEN 1 END) AS query_executions, COUNT(CASE WHEN e.object_type = 'query' AND e.action IN ('view', 'view_source') THEN 1 END) AS query_views, COU

title: Proposal: Isolate Query Runners to Simplify Dependency Management · getredash/redash · Discussion #7601 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I was thinking we could split out the less-used query runners — especially those with awkward or conflicting dependencies — into separate virtual environments inside the Docker container, and invoke them as subprocesses. These runners could be bundled as independent packages or even standalone projects, with a very simple, well-defined protocol for execution. That would let us effectively stop maintaining their dependency trees in the main Redash codebase. Down the line, this also opens the door to non-Python runners if someone prefers that. Upsides • Much simpler dependency management for the core codebase • Easier and safer automated dependency upgrades on a regular cadence • Smaller memory footprint for the main processes Downsides • Added latency from spawning a subprocess per query • Some upfront complexity in designing and wiring the protocol Overall, this feels like a reasonable tradeoff to reduce long-term maintenance pain and isolate problematic dependencies.
author: arikfr
comment_excerpt: It looks a quite interesting idea ! At first, I think we can refer how other BI tools handle datasources. Superset It looks superset delegate dependency issue to SQLalchemy and DB-API python database driver. https://superset.apache.org/docs/configuration/databases/ Metabase It looks Metabase provides JAR based plugin driver mechanism. https://www.metabase.com/docs/latest/developers-guide/community-drivers And, I think that there are topics we can think about. Streaming v.s Store-and-Forward Currently, Redash stores all the result to the memory, and send it to client. If we just simply split query runner to two processes(main and sub), we may need double or triple memory space for storing the data(main process, sub process, and possibly communication pipe). Handling docker images for each r || @arikfr note that it's not a separate container, but a separate virtual env. Ah, I see. Thank you ! And, there are also some questions in my mind. Should we support the data sources which uses other virtual env ? Should we set up CI to run the test for the data sources with different virtual env ? Thank you ! || Should we support the data sources which uses other virtual env ? I think that we can let other people (who actually use them) support and maintain them. It's sort of like it is today, but more explicit. Should we set up CI to run the test for the data sources with different virtual env ? The nice thing is that we can test the integration point but don't have to test the individual packages. || @arikfr I think that we can let other people (who actually use them) support and maintain them. It's sort of like it is today, but more explicit. In my understanding, as of today, all the data source is maintained by us, maintainers, and not other people . (As, at least one maintainer need to approve the PR.) || Formally it's all maintained by us. But in practice, do you test every single one of them? || @arikfr For me, until now, I approved PRs only when I or other maintainer tested. I feel it is not necessary and I'll approve that I have no testing environment. But at least I'll approve only when I fully understand the source code and feel good enough. And, I'm just wondering who is the "other people". Are you thinking to stop supporting data sources which have no "other people" who commit to maintain and develop the data source ? For now, I think some data sources have intermittent contributor but no active maintainer. At now, I think those are somehow working, I'm just wondering we should stop supporting those data sources...

title: Local Data processing · getredash/redash · Discussion #7569 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi Redash community, I have a requirement to process data locally before visualizing it. There is a certain type of logic which is not very straightforward to write in query language. But if I fetch the required data from DB, and have an option to pre process it before rendering using local js scripts, it would be very helpful. I have explored the option of custom charts, where I can write custom js functions, but confined to use Plotly. If that can be extended to just return the processed data and can use Redash native charts as it is, it should solve the purpose I believe. Do you folks have this type of requirement, or any other alternatives to solve this kind of problem?
author: parasjindal-tf
comment_excerpt: This is interesting. The main issue is security concerns... need to somehow sandbox the execution. || I have created my own fork for this and added a new js script option before rendering. In the sandbox env, I have set various parameters of default js as undefined like document, axios etc. You can check this PR - parasjindal-tf#1 This is working for me. || Thank you for the follow up and sharing! From past experience homegrown sandboxing can be bypassed... In your case it can be bypassed with the following: // This escapes your shadow variables entirely const actualGlobalThis = (new Function('return this'))(); actualGlobalThis.alert("I escaped!"); Another concern is that it blocks the main UI loop (in case it does some heavy processing, or just a while(true) loop). Gemini suggested to delegate the processing to a web worker, which will provide isolation and ability to kill it if it doesn't respond in time. The other suggestions were ShadowRealm (but that's in proposal stage and not yet fully supported) or run it in a WASM QuickJS runtime. || Got it. Thanks for quick revert. I understand the concern and believe if this functionality is executed in a same way as Plotly, it would open numerous possibilities. I am already using my fork build for our internal dashboarding and this has been a game changer. Single query is powering multiple charts in a same dashboard.

title: Allow setting a default number format · getredash/redash · Discussion #7139 · GitHub

I'd love to change the setting all at once rather than editing the visualization for every query, column-by-column.

comment_excerpt: Where you able to solve this? I'm interested in the same || No — this is a feature request. || Same for me, I'm using it mostly to work with IDs, so it's very inconvenient to change number format all the time || This feature is missing in the UI, but it can bet set in the database UPDATE organizations SET settings = jsonb_set(settings::jsonb, ' {settings,float_format} ' , ' "0.0" ' , true); UPDATE organizations SET settings = jsonb_set(settings::jsonb, ' {settings,integer_format} ' , ' "0" ' , true); SELECT jsonb_pretty(settings::jsonb) FROM organizations; || Interesting, I'll give it a try || Please be aware of the bug #2666 Integer numbers that greater then 2^53 can not be displayed correctly in JavaScript.

title: suppress logging the /ping health checks · getredash/redash · Discussion #7566 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I've deployed the redash/redash:25.1.0 image as an ECS service in AWS. The configuration is very standard: I do not override the container command, and I just set the env vars containing DB, Redis, and the cookie and key secret. The container port is the standard 5000. There's an ALB in front of the service. The ALB health check hits the container /ping endpoint every so often. If the service is usually quiet, the health checks overwhelm the useful logs in CloudWatch. I do not want to see the GET /ping events in the logs - if the service is healthy then it is declared healthy by the ALB, and if it's not healthy then I will see those events elsewhere in AWS. So those log entries are useless noise. Is there a way to tell the Redash server to log all HTTP requests except for this one path?
author: FlorinAndrei

title: Support more Flask Rate Limiter Configuration · getredash/redash · Discussion #7563 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: There is currently a minimal configuration of the Flask Rate Limiter plugin available via environment variables. However, this does not fully encapsulate what the plugin can do. Most importantly in our environment, we run the application behind a load balancer. Because of that, all requests come in from the same IP address. To find the true client IP, the X-Forwarded-For header must be inspected. The plugin seems to support this, see Deploying an application behind a proxy . However, we have no capacity to enable that within Redash as it is currently deployed. I would suggest expanding the list of environment variables to provide more flexibility of the rate limiter.
author: phillipjohnson
comment_excerpt: @phillipjohnson How about setting REDASH_PROXIES_COUNT environment variable to set the number of proxies(default value is 1) between Flask and the client ? || Ah, I missed that one, will take a look!

title: Developing with Docker and inotify with Flask on M1 MacOS - extremely slow! · getredash/redash · Discussion #7515 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I had a quirky thing today where Flask would take roughly 11-13 minutes to boot - extremely slow and annoying! Basically I couldn't get anything done. Turns out it was inotify issues where it Flask would deadlock on a particular folder in the container such as /app . This only seemed to affect an M1 MacBook, not a traditional Intel MacBook from a few years ago. I switched to the Docker VMM virtualisation (away from Apple Virtualization ) in Docker and this seemed to do the trick - for now! The other option was to remove watchdog from pyproject.toml or use a proper production server (such as gunicorn ) that doesn't use the file monitoring / watching. Hope this helps someone else one day!
author: wtfiwtz
comment_excerpt: Thank you for your information ! I myself don't see the problem on the latest Redash / Docker Desktop with Apple Virtualization on M4 MacBook Pro...

body_or_excerpt: It seems like alerts are always based on comparing returned values to a reference point. However, I want to have alerts based on whether a query returns rows. If it returns any rows, there's a problem and the alert should be triggered. If no rows are returned, there's no problem and the alert should be considered OK. title: Really basic question from a noob re: getting set up locally with Docker · getredash/redash · Discussion #7476 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi all, I should say off the bat that I find Redash quite intimidating as I'm no sys-admin. My aim is to get it running locally with Docker. To that end, I've done the following: Created docker-compose.yml and given it the exact same contents as the suggested compose file in the Redash GH repo Started Docker Desktop Run docker compose up This gives me an error about failing to read dockerfile. Now, I know, I know, it must be blindingly obvious to someone reading this what I'm doing wrong, but Redash is presumably intended for sys-admins and assumes the reader has all sorts of knowledge, and so didn't explicitly say I needed to create a dockerfile first, or what to put in it. To this end, I'd be hugely grateful if someone could just point me in the right direction as to what I need to do! Thank you in advance.
author: mitya33
comment_excerpt: The file in the repo is a template, and the image name is being replaced dynamically by the setup script. If you're running it manually, you’ll need to set it explicitly. The latest stable version should be image: redash/redash:v25.1.0 . Also, note that it tries to load an .env file from this path: /opt/redash/env ( see line 6 ). You can change that to wherever your environment file is located. Lastly, on line 44 , it sets the Postgres data volume path — you might want to update that too, depending on your setup. Once you've made those 3 adjustments, running docker compose up should work. || Hi, I'm also trying to set up a demo instance of redash on a Windows dev machine under docker. I tried to follow the instructions at https://redash.io/help/open-source/setup/ I am new to Docker and (obviously) to redash but got a few other reporting things running in Docker with few problems (e.g. Metabase). The link in the setup instructions to the "docker image" links to a page with a "Run in Docker Desktop" button rather than an image directly. That button seemed to work in that it created the docker container, etc. but it does not run... I get an exception "You must set the REDASH_COOKIE_SECRET environment variable". The setup page does say that a secret key and a cookie secret key are needed "if you do not use one of our cloud images", so I thought I might not need to do that. However || Do you want to just run a Redash instance locally or do you need a dev setup? || Thanks for the response... I'm trying to investigate it for possible use on a project so I think I just need a local demo/vanilla instance of some kind that I can play with... to start with at least. If it looks promising for what we need, then we'd need dev setups at that stage. || I created a simple Docker Compose setup you can run locally. I tried it only on MacOS, but I believe it should work on Windows as well. Save the following as a compose.yaml file: x-redash-service : &redash-service image : redash/redash:25.8.0 depends_on : - postgres - redis restart : always x-redash-environment : &redash-environment REDASH_HOST : http://localhost:5001 REDASH_LOG_LEVEL : " INFO " PYTHONUNBUFFERED : " 0 " REDASH_REDIS_URL : " redis://redis:6379/0 " REDASH_DATABASE_URL : " postgresql://postgres@postgres/postgres " REDASH_ENFORCE_CSRF : " true " REDASH_GUNICORN_TIMEOUT : " 60 " # Replace the following with actual secrets REDASH_COOKIE_SECRET : SOMETHING_SECRET REDASH_SECRET_KEY : ANOTHER_SECRET services : database_setup : : *redash-service command : create_db depends_on : - po || Thank you so much! That worked... although I got no response on localhost:5001 I used localhost:5001/setup and was able to get started and connect my data source... I'm up and running. Thanks again - really appreciate your help and time on this.

title: Adding GenAI support to Redash · getredash/redash · Discussion #6733 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Just testing the waters to check if adding Generative AI-based features to Redash is under consideration!? Here are some ideas, Auto-generate Individual Queries : As Redash has access to tbl and their schema, it should be possible to auto-generate queries based on either the title or comment of the query or provided prompt. Auto-complete Individual Queries : Given a partial query and its comment or prompt, it should be possible to complete the query. Suggest Title for Queries : Based on the queries in the Query page, we can suggest title. Add comments : User can select part or complete queries and AI can add comments for better explainability. Error Debugging : Based on the executed query and the error, AI can suggest more verbose error mitigation steps. Edit existing queries : User can select part or complete queries, and provide prompt to modify the query. Ex: "add one more case to this query" or "simplify the query" And all of these functionalities could be supported by connecting to OpenAI (or other) 3rd party GenAI service provider. User can set their OpenAI keys in the Settings page. Please share your thoughts and add more ideas if I am missing anything. Thanks!
author: imohitmayank
comment_excerpt: I don't think this would be safe from an opsec standpoint. OpenAI would get to know the schema of all of the data available to the user. The other suggestions (title, error help) don't seem like they'd be worth the effort of implementing, to be honest. || This could be an optional setting so only interested users can opt-in. OpenAI supports an opt-out policy, so you can ask them to not use your data. Also, this is a list of potential ideas and it might make sense for different use cases, for example people just getting started with SQL might find it useful to have AI powered error-debugging feature. || I agree with this not being an opsec concern, lots of other products are embedding Azure OpenAI for some of these for example. It would be opt-in for sure, as each org would configure their key only when they want to have those features (would consider it by org, not by user because of the 3rd party data though). || Example of how flow works for a browser extension intended for Metabsae: https://avanty.app/ || This is good, thanks for sharing. Added some missing ideas to the original list. || have you tried the copycat's https://github.com/DeepInsight-AI/DeepBI ? they just gave redash a face-lift and added gen-ai support 😹

title: Setup SAML2 on Okta · getredash/redash · Discussion #7482 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, We are trying to set up Redash SAML in Okta; however, it isn't working as expected. We used this document: https://redash.io/help/user-guide/users/authentication-options/ We are getting this error message: Internal Server Error The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application. Could someone please advise us on how to resolve this issue? Thank you so much
author: siamakjafari2024
comment_excerpt: @siamakjafari2024 I haven't used Redash with Okta, but I think you can check redash server log, as s starting point. || This was working with Auth0 on Redash 10.1.0 but I've encountered an issue with Redash 25.07-dev where the signature validation now fails. Note that I had made some minor changes to the Python library versions (to further support ARM64 on MacOS) and moved to Python 3.9 which might be related. The error is global component \\\'xs:string\\\' not found It might be possible to customize the XML metadata presented by the IdP, or look at getting pysaml2 to ignore this error somehow. Here's the environment variables we were using on Redash 10.1.0 REDASH_SAML_SSO_URL: "true", // Used as a flag to enable SAML login REDASH_SAML_NAMEID_FORMAT: "emailAddress", REDASH_JWT_AUTH_ISSUER: ""https://idp-host.co/", REDASH_JWT_AUTH_PUBLIC_CERTS_URL: "https://idp-host.co/.well-known/jwks.json", REDASH_JWT_AUTH || Looks like my issue might be due to a recent change in xmlschema - sissaschool/xmlschema#452 I'm testing a revert to v3.3.0 My branch is https://github.com/orchestrated-io/redash/commits/25.07.0-dev-py39/ || Ekkk. Looks like the master branch is already on Python 3.10 but I branched off the 25.07.0-dev snapshot tag, which doesn't include commits from upstream master as they are added in the forked repo (from a Github Action). Looks like I need to go back and start from the latest commits. || Looks like the 2025.01 release also fails on ARM (in AWS Fargate). Similar error again: xmlschema.exceptions.XMLSchemaKeyError: "global xs:simpleType/xs:complexType 'xs:string' not found" (in File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/xmlschema/validators/global_maps.py", line 271) Might be the flavour of SAML supported by Auth0. You can disable typedAttributes in the Auth0 SAML2 plugin settings, and maybe customize some parts of the SAML response on redirect, but not during the initial SP=>IdP=>SP dance AFAIK. https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/protocols/saml/saml-configuration/customize-saml-assertions I'll have to keep playing around. There's also a possibility it is only an issue on ARM. The Redash logs are also referring to Python 3.9 which doesn't seem right. || At some point in recent months xmlschema v4 had a fallback map - sissaschool/xmlschema@ 01304a0 But it's subsequently been removed with the new Safe Schema loader commit 🤔 - sissaschool/xmlschema@ 4eb5fd0 . There is a comment about problems with assertions too that might require a higher version of elementpath - sissaschool/xmlschema#431 (comment) In the 2025.01 release, the poetry.lock seems to have version = "3.0.1" but the specification is >=1.2.1 from pysaml2 (since 6.5.2). The 10.1.0 release was previously on pysaml2 6.1.0 (I don't believe this used xmlschema yet). https://github.com/IdentityPython/pysaml2/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md#610-2020-07-10 pysaml2 6.5.0 seems to have introduced the xmlschema requirement to fix the main pysaml2 security vulnerability - https://github.com/Identity || Ok that looks like it worked. I also had to lock numpy and use a non-binary release (through an update to the Dockerfile ) as well. https://github.com/orchestrated-io/redash/commits/25.01-release/

title: Support for nested drop downs is much needed! · getredash/redash · Discussion #6744 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: This is a copy of: https://discuss.redash.io/t/observable-query-based-dropdown-list/3229 Redash is such a great tool 🎉 - however, one thing I really lack is nested query based drop downs. Basically the idea is, that if your database has two columns, say: country, city then those could be used to populate two nested drop downs. One for country, one for city. However currently, that is not possible because the queries powering drop downs cannot interdepend: Example of what we need You could have a query like: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM my_db . my_table ORDER BY country Then add a "Query Based Drop Down" with those countries Then use the selected variable in another drop down query for city: SELECT DISTINCT city FROM my_db . my_table WHERE country = ' {{country}} ' ORDER BY city It is such a pain this functionality does not exist 😬
author: NielsKSchjoedt
comment_excerpt: Any update?????

title: Ability to customize Counter look · getredash/redash · Discussion #7176 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, I was wondering if there is any means to eachieve a small degree of customization for the counters, like the ability to specify the font color I'm a new Redash user and I'm missing most of the context. I think the fact that Counter widget doesn't support HTML has been mentioned a few times by the maintainers, for example here . I'd be happy to contribute with a PR, I just wanted to hear out what our maintainers think about it.
author: cezudas
comment_excerpt: Pinging @eradman – maybe current maintainers have no opinion about it. At the very least if not HTML support, we'd like to be able to make the counter support a custom hyperlink. || The ability to customize the counter widget would be useful. I haven't given it much thought, but HTML support seems reasonable. || comparing with the Table viz, it seems that Counter could be rationalized. note in Table you can use Display as: Number and specify formatting, whereas in Counter it has a dedicated tab. we could give Counter a Display as: Text type with optional HTML support, Number which enables the same features under Format, and Link for Redash-style hyperlink configuration. It seems that if Redash doesn't detect a number in the data, all the fields under the Format tab are disabled anyway. || Also, instead of Formatting Decimal Place Formatting Decimal Character Formatting Thousands Separator Formatting String Prefix Formatting String Suffix We should just support a Number format the same way that Table does! || Yes exactly, these could all be rolled up under a Display as: Number feature. I imagine Table evolved whereas was left in its more primitive, original design. Or someone decided it should only ever display a number and therefore breaking out the formatting makes sense.

title: Redash Helm Chart · getredash/redash · Discussion #7445 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi Everybody I've published a Helm chart for deploying Redash 25.1.0 on Kubernetes, optimized for workloads and designed with Azure-native environments in mind. Repository: https://github.com/thcp/redash Key Features Support for Redash 25.1.0 on Kubernetes 1.21+ with Helm 3.2.0+ Modular deployment of Redash Web, Worker, and Scheduler PostgreSQL and Redis included by default, with option to disable for external backends HPA enabled for workers based on CPU/memory usage Ingress + TLS support SAML 2.0 authentication tested with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) Secure and scalable out of the box Fully configurable via values.yaml I hope this can be useful like it was for my on my personal projects :)

title: Snowflake Will Block Single-Factor Password Authentication · getredash/redash · Discussion #7273 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: snowflake plans to make MFA authentication mandatory. It would be helpful if you could also support connection authentication with redash. Thank you in advance. https://www.snowflake.com/en/blog/blocking-single-factor-password-authentification/
author: nishimurakaori
comment_excerpt: I suggest implementing key-pair authentication because implementing MFA in Redash would be challenging, so adding support for key-pair authentication would be a better approach. || Hi there, Luis from Snowflake. While Key-pair authentication is an option. For human interactive scenarios, I would encourage Redash team to support OAuth 2.0 Authorization code flow ( Snowflake OAuth ). In this way, users doesn't have to invest in maintaining individual keys for this integration. || Thanks for the good ideas. But we can't use oAuth2.0 in our company for a while. Please let me know if there is another way. Thank you in advance. || A workaround is to set the Snowflake user to type LEGACY_SERVICE alter user REDASH_DEV set TYPE = 'LEGACY_SERVICE' https://docs.snowflake.com/en/sql-reference/sql/create-user#label-user-type-property I'm not sure yet how long this will be supported by Snowflake. @sfc-gh-lleon do you perhaps have some information about this? || It seems that users of TYPE = LEGACY_SERVICE will be migrated to TYPE = SERVICE users by November 2025. https://www.snowflake.com/en/blog/blocking-single-factor-password-authentification/ || As described in this article, I was able to authenticate users with TYPE = SERVICE by building a custom image with a Snowflake data source that supports Key-Pair authentication. https://zenn.dev/dataheroes/articles/20250411-redash-snowflake-keypair-authn

title: Redash and Oracle via Wallet · getredash/redash · Discussion #7343 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi, I need to configure redash to connect to oracle DB via wallet in the Oracle Cloud. When executing sqlplus in the container running reddish server I am able to connect but not when configuring the data source in redash. Did anyone have success to configure oracle connections via Wallets? I created container like this: ` FROM redash/redash:latest USER root Oracle instantclient ADD oracle/instantclient-basic-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip /tmp/instantclient-basic-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip ADD oracle/instantclient-sdk-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip /tmp/instantclient-sdk-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip ADD oracle/instantclient-sqlplus-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip /tmp/instantclient-sqlplus-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip ADD oracle/Wallet.zip /tmp/Wallet.zip RUN apt-get update -y RUN apt-get install -y unzip RUN unzip /tmp/instantclient-basic-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip -d /usr/local/ RUN unzip -o /tmp/instantclient-sdk-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip -d /usr/local/ RUN unzip -o /tmp/instantclient-sqlplus-linux.x64-19.26.0.0.0dbru.zip -d /usr/local/ RUN ln -s /usr/local/instantclient_19_26 /usr/local/instantclient RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/instantclient/network/admin RUN unzip -o /tmp/Wallet.zip -d /usr/local/instantclient/network/admin ADD oracle/sqlnet.ora /usr/local/instantclient/network/admin/sqlnet.ora #RUN ln -s /usr/local/instantclient/libclntsh.so.19.1 /usr/local/instantclient/libclntsh.so RUN ln -s /usr/local/instantclient/sqlplus /usr/bin/sqlplus RUN apt-get install libaio-dev -y RUN apt-get clean -y ENV ORACLE_HOME=/usr/local/instantclient ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$LD_LIBRARY_PATH:/usr/local/instantclient ENV TNS_ADMIN=/usr/local/instantclient/network/admin RUN pip install cx_Oracle==7.0.0 USER redash #Add REDASH ENV to add Oracle Query Runner ENV REDASH_ADDITIONAL_QUERY_RUNNERS=redash.query_runner.oracle ` Thanks Michael
author: hoebling
comment_excerpt: @hoebling Did anyone have success configuring Oracle connections via Wallets? If you are using Docker , you can use the following tags to get a version that includes the necessary changes: FROM redash/redash:preview or FROM redash/redash:25.1.0 For more details, check the available tags here: 🔗 Redash Docker Hub Tags With these versions, Oracle Client and Wallet are not required when setting up a new Redash server. This is because the following pull requests have already been merged: Do not makedsn for query_runner/oracle.py #6332 Enabled support for Oracle Cloud by allowing DSN Service Name to accept Oracle Cloud connection strings Replace cx_Oracle with python-oracledb #6386 Replaced Oracle Instant Client with python-oracledb , making Oracle Instant Client unnecessary Add redash.query_ru || @hoebling It’s great to hear that you can configure the host via DNS! Regarding the timeout issue, could you provide more details about your Oracle Database environment? Which Oracle Database environment are you using? Oracle Cloud or something else? If Oracle Cloud, is it Autonomous Database or Base Database ? How are you connecting? Over the public internet or a private connection ? These details will help narrow down the cause of the timeout issue. 🚀 || Hi, it is oracle cloud Autonomous DB using private connection. Maybe there is a way to increase logging to see what is done while waiting. Thanks Michael || @hoebling OK, got it. You're using an Autonomous Database , so I'll explain the setup for Autonomous Transaction Processing (ATP) . How to Connect to an Autonomous Database If Mutual TLS (mTLS) Authentication is Not Required No Wallet is needed. No need to change the Redash Docker image . Log in to the Autonomous Database console . Select your Autonomous Database instance . Click the "DB Connection" tab. Select TLS authentication: "TLS" , then copy the connection string . In Redash, go to Settings → Data Source . Click "+ New Data Source" → Select "Oracle" . Enter a name (e.g., ATP ). Enter the User and Password for the database. Set "Host" to _useservicename (this is a reserved keyword). Set "Port" to 1521 . Paste the Connection String into "DSN Service Name" . Example: (description=(retr || Hi, thank you for the detailed explanation. Except for the ip address to be configured in tnsnames.ora I have had the setup already before. Error is still {"message": "Task exceeded maximum timeout value (30 seconds)}. I created small python script inside server container with cx_Oracle and python-oracledb and python-oracledb only works with oracledb.init_oracle_client(lib_dir="/usr/local/instantclient/") set. Any further suggestions? Thanks Michael || @hoebling Network Connectivity Check Inside the Container Using Telnet (if installed) telnet [your private endpoint ip address] 1521 If you see Connected to ... , the connection is successful. If you get Connection refused or Connection timed out , there might be a network or NSG issue. Using netcat (nc) (if nc is not available) Install nc if missing: apt update && apt install -y netcat Then, check the connection: nc -zv [your private endpoint ip address] 1521 If succeeded appears, the connection is successful. If Connection refused or Connection timed out , check network or NSG settings. 🚨 Please confirm the result of either nc or telnet . This is essential to diagnose the issue. 🚨 Confirming Whether TLS or mTLS is Used You can check this in the OCI console: Log in to OCI Open your Autono || Using redash:preview or redash:25.1.0 I am getting following error [2025-03-13 17:11:00,881][PID:10][INFO][xmlschema] Resource 'XMLSchema.xsd' is already loaded [2025-03-13 17:11:13,190][PID:7][INFO][metrics] method=GET path=/ endpoint=redash_index status=302 content_type=text/html; charset=utf-8 content_length=329 duration=0.72 query_count=2 query_duration=9.47 [2025-03-13 17:11:13,625][PID:10][INFO][metrics] method=GET path=/login endpoint=redash_login status=302 content_type=text/html; charset=utf-8 content_length=199 duration=3.17 query_count=2 query_duration=8.38 [2025-03-13 17:11:13,761][PID:10][INFO][metrics] method=GET path=/setup endpoint=redash_setup status=200 content_type=text/html; charset=utf-8 content_length=2829 duration=15.59 query_count=2 query_duration=2.71 [2025-03-13 1 || @rizwan-ishtiaq The error ValueError: Attribute 'settings' does not accept objects of type suggests that an invalid value (a string) has been set for settings . Since this setting is entered via the web configuration page, please check the following: Ensure that the value entered for settings is of the correct type. If possible, try re-entering the value to see if the issue persists. Additionally, if you could share the value you entered in the settings page, it may help identify the issue more specifically.

title: Dynamic Addition of Query Runners in Redash Without Rebuilding (Beginner Question) · getredash/redash · Discussion #7183 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: I'm new to Redash and still learning about its architecture and capabilities. As I explore its features, I'm curious about the possibility of extending its functionality by adding new query runners dynamically. From what I understand so far, adding a new query runner typically requires modifying the core codebase and rebuilding the application. However, I'm wondering if there's a more flexible approach. My question is: Is there a way for a Redash beginner to add new query runners dynamically at runtime, without having to rebuild and redeploy the entire application? Specifically, I'd like to know: Are there any existing user-friendly mechanisms or plugins in Redash that allow for dynamic addition of query runners? If not, what would be the recommended approach for a Redash newcomer to implement or request such functionality? What are the potential challenges or security considerations that a beginner should be aware of when thinking about this kind of feature? As someone new to Redash, I'm open to learning about any existing solutions, workarounds, or even alternative approaches that might address this need. Any beginner-friendly insights or explanations would be greatly appreciated.
author: johnthotekat
comment_excerpt: Query runners are essentially python code that sends raw query texts to a database. Each database has some unique aspects to which the python code needs to adapt. Following best practices, Redash maintainers and contributors should thoroughly test their runner code. From a security perspective, all accesses to the db should be managed upon the creation of the db users. AFAIK, Redash has little way or responsibility to prevent a DROP ALL DATABASES from being executed. Some Redash users do use it for good with admin privileges. || Thanks @zachliu for the context. I understand that the query runners are python code. Which is placed up here : https://github.com/getredash/redash/tree/master/redash/query_runner . And I was thinking of writing a custom query runner, which would be inhertiting BaseHTTPQueryRunner. To write and test the query runner, building redash again and deploying becomes a productivity killer. I'm keenly interested to know if we've some way to dynamically link the query runner custom class to redash. || Ah, I thought you meant dynamically adding runners in a production environment, which requires a fundamental change on Redash. Follow this guide , your dev environment will be able to load whatever code changes dynamically. || This seems strange, it would be incredibly useful (and simple) to be able to dynamically specify query_runners. This can easily be done and solve 90% of users' issues by simply updating the docker version to "accept" a volume where it can check for dynamic query_runners, and simply copy them in. The onus would be on the users to correctly create their runners and add them to the volume, which is perfect. Then there is the case of the URL query_runner ( here ). This query_runner would also solve the problem of anyone who is trying to dynamically add some query_runner to get some data into Redash, because they can set up an API that would return the data in the correct format. However, it is not visible on the front end, even if you have REDASH_ADDITIONAL_QUERY_RUNNERS: "redash.query_runner.

We also found this post from 2023 [ link ] indicating the project's revival. This is fantastic news! Given this renewed activity, we have some questions regarding Redash's future: Long-Term Vision and Roadmap : Could the community team share the long-term vision and roadmap for Redash? Understanding the project's direction will help us assess whether it aligns with our organization's BI needs. Security Updates : Can we expect to continue receiving security upgrades for Redash? title: Redash memory consumption is too high · getredash/redash · Discussion #5971 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Deployed: AWS EC2 How: Dcoker-Compose Ram: 64GB Its frequently happening that memory usage suddenly steeps high upto max 64GB and EC2 Instance goes unreachable. How can we prevent this issue?
author: aryankumar04
comment_excerpt: Ahhh, that's a bit too generic of a problem report for really figuring out what's wrong. How often are you experiencing the problem? eg "once every few weeks" vs "once every few hours" (etc) Generally the thing that causes high memory use is starting up too many worker processes. With current versions of redash, each worker process seems to take about 1/4 of a GB of unshared RAM. So, if you're starting up too many then it can go badly. From your description though, it sounds more like memory use is ok for a bit... then is going suddenly very large. That sounds more like a memory leak, or someone's running some kind of uncontained process. Not sure what though. 😦

title: query snippets to simplify queries · getredash/redash · Discussion #5952 · GitHub
body_or_excerpt: Hi there, Our current approach for data ingestion suggests importing data on a daily basis with the full load. It means that to query data from such tables, I need to use filters to get data with max load_date. I've included an example of such a query below. As you can see, it's hard to read and maintain. Could you please advise how I can use query snippets in Redash to enable data analysts to filter data for max load_date in a more convenient way? SELECT t1.id, t1.column1 AS FXE_Number, CAST(date_format(t1.column2, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%i:%s') AS varchar) AS Creation_Date, t1.column3 AS Email_Subject, CAST(date_format(t1.column4, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%i:%s') AS varchar) AS Close_Date, t1.column5 AS Sender_ADDRESS, t2.column6 AS Category_Title, t3.column7 AS Activity, t4.login_name, t5.name AS State, Floor(date_diff('day', t1.column2, t1.column4)) AS AHT_day FROM test_table1 t1 LEFT JOIN test_table2 t2 ON t1.column8 = t2.request AND date(t2.column9) = (SELECT MAX(date(column9)) FROM test_table2) LEFT JOIN test_table3 t3 ON t1.column10 = t3.id AND date(t3.column11) = (SELECT MAX(date(column11)) FROM test_table3) LEFT JOIN test_table4 t4 ON t1.column12 = t4.id AND date(t4.load_date) = (SELECT MAX(date(load_date)) FROM test_table4) LEFT JOIN test_table5 t5 ON t1.column13 = t5.id AND date(t5.load_date) = (SELECT MAX(date(load_date)) FROM test_table5) WHERE t1.column4 BETWEEN TIMESTAMP '2023-02-10' AND TIMESTAMP '2023-02-12' ORDER BY t1.id DESC;
